oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Estimation of T. foetus organisms present in field samples using stochastic simulations
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Jumper, T.M.
Thoresen, M.
King, E.H.
Loy, D.
Smith, D.R.
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Tritrichomonas foetus, a protozoan parasite that is transmitted from the bull host to cows during coitus, can cause reproduc­tive failure and is regulated at the state level. The newly de­veloped direct RT-rtPCR provides potential advantages over previously used diagnostic modalities. The reported limit of detection of the RT-rtPCR is 1 T. foetus organism/100 μL, with a decrease in detectable RNA in samples that have < 10 organ­isms/100 μL. It is generally believed that T. foetus-infected bulls would have much higher levels of organisms in a preputial sam­ple, however, research is lacking. Therefore, the objective of this study was to estimate the number of T. foetus organisms in field samples submitted for direct RT-rtPCR testing.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 232
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
